title: openbindings.operation-graph execution fixture file
description: Describes one or more execution fixtures for the openbindings.operation-graph format. Each fixture pairs a graph, a set of mocked operation behaviors, and the stream of caller writes with the expected output stream, so a runner can replay the graph deterministically and compare. Mocks are matched per invocation: an `operation` conduit opens one invocation receiving every arriving event as a write; an `each` node opens one single-write invocation per arriving event. This schema constrains fixture shape only; a runner validates each `graph` against the op-graph schema separately.

section.description: The spec section or example this fixture is drawn from.

operations.description: Mocked behavior for each operation an `operation` or `each` node may invoke, keyed by operation key.

closesAfter.description: The selected binding closes the invocation's input side after reading this many writes (1 models a unary or server-streaming binding), at which point the invocation's outputs are determined and emitted and a holding conduit becomes non-accepting. Absent models a stream-consuming (client-streaming/bidirectional) binding: input stays open until the node closes it, and outputs are emitted at input completion.

responses.description: Response rules, matched in order against a completed invocation's write list. The first rule whose `whenInputs` deep-equals the write list, or whose `whenInput` deep-equals the sole write of a single-write invocation, or which carries neither (a wildcard), applies.

whenInput.description: Matches an invocation whose writes are exactly [this value]. Sugar for the common single-write case (`each` invocations, unary conduits).

whenInputs.description: Matches an invocation whose write list deep-equals this array. An empty array matches a no-input invocation (zero writes).

emit.description: Events the invocation emits when matched. More than one element models a streaming output.

fail.description: If present, the invocation terminates with this error instead of emitting. At an `each` node this is a per-event failure; at an `operation` conduit it is a terminal error — fatal to the graph unless the node sets onError.

graph.description: An operation graph definition. A runner validates this against the op-graph JSON Schema.

writes.description: The values the caller writes to the graph invocation, in order; each becomes one event at the input node and roots a lineage. An empty array is a no-input invocation. The caller closes the input side after the last write (unless back-closure closes it first).

output.description: Expected operation-graph output events (events reaching the output node, plus any error:false exit event).

ordering.description: `exact` requires the output array to match in order; `set` requires the same multiset of events, since concurrency makes the order implementation-defined.

arrayOrdering.description: Applies to array-valued output events. `exact` (default) compares array elements positionally; `set` compares them as a multiset, for a collector (e.g. a buffer) fed by concurrent `each` invocations or fan-in paths whose element order the spec's "Determinism and portability" section leaves implementation-defined. Distinct from `ordering`, which governs the order of the output-event stream itself.

error.description: True when the graph is expected to terminate with an error: an exit node with error:true, or an unhandled terminal error on an operation conduit.

errorDetail.description: When error is true, the expected error detail: the event that reached the exit node, or for an unhandled conduit terminal error, the inner terminal error value itself (the value direct invocation would surface; not wrapped in the error-event shape).
